page: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the EDSv4 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz, featuring 32 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes 35 MB of L3 cache and supports instruction set extensions such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 1229 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data positions this instance primarily in the average performance tier for compute and memory operations, with a notable exception in memory performance. The PassMark memory score is rated as strong, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems, which correlates with a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 1663.39 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 29820.32 and a PassMark CPU score of 27501.47. Database and key-value store operations are supported by a Redis SET throughput of 27.54 million operations per second. However, large-scale LLM workloads show limitations, with the 70B parameter model prompt processing speed registering in the bottom 25% at 5.59 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32ds_v4 offers a balanced resource density for memory-intensive workloads without the premium cost of specialized bare-metal hardware. The inclusion of 1229 GB of local storage reduces the immediate need for external storage attachments for temporary data. However, the lack of hardware acceleration limits its utility for heavy machine learning training or real-time large language model generation. This server is suited for memory-optimized workloads, relational databases, enterprise applications, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 2.6 GHz clock speed. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 1229 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show average compute capabilities, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 27501.47, but strong memory performance with a PassMark memory score in the top 25%. While the instance exhibits limitations in large-scale LLM inference, it delivers solid throughput for database operations, with Redis SET performance reaching 27.54 million operations per second. This profile makes it suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v4 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth for databases and enterprise applications.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v4 is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, suited for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-density RAM and local storage for demanding database and enterprise application workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
1229 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
